title: Sanji’s Childhood Wound
description: On pages 11–14, this part focuses on Unknown person #9, Unknown person #10, Unknown person #11, Luffy, Unknown person #12, Nami as Sanji’s childhood trauma and the political trap around his marriage collide with Luffy’s refusal to abandon him.
scene: Sanji’s past explains why his family name is a wound rather than a source of pride.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How many times do I have to tell you...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: I can't take down another crumb!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: So even you have a limit.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But that doesn't mean... I'll go throw up and waste what I've already eaten!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'll do you one better! If I fill that oversized stomach of yours with holes... I'm sure I'll help relieve some of that stress!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm going to end this... before you get any more funny ideas!!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I must say, that's a stark contrast from your initial transfer- nation!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Can you even move in TahT state?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Don't spray any water on the remaining biscuits!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: It's time you felt the power...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Enjoy your trip to the ends of the new world!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He bit off more than he could chew! It's Luffy's new gear 4th!

summary: This section advances the Sanji and the Vinsmokes storyline with 37 dialogue beats. Sanji’s family trauma and the forced wedding turn the rescue mission into an emotional test of loyalty.
